policy.note: A source is download-eligible only when matches_text_translation=true, music_free=true, license is set/acceptable, audio_url is set, and approved=true. Otherwise the audit reports the source as 'needs_verification' and (when nothing is approved) surfaces sources/alternatives.json. Never download a near-match. To enable a source: confirm at the listed portal that the audio records the SAME translation as the text and that its license permits use, then set matches_text_translation/license/audio_url and approved=true.

sources.audio_provider: Faith Comes By Hearing — Non-Drama (single narrator, music-free)
sources.evidence: Text is the open Door43 ULB. FCBH publishes a music-free Non-Drama Swahili NT, but it may record a different Swahili translation than the ULB; exact-text-match and audio license must be confirmed at the FCBH portal before any download.

sources.text_title: Alkitab Gratis untuk Semua (Bible for All)
sources.audio_provider: Faith Comes By Hearing — Non-Drama (single narrator, music-free)
sources.evidence: Open Indonesian text with broad audio coverage. The FCBH Non-Drama Indonesian NT is music-free but its translation identity vs. the AGS text and its license need confirmation before download.

audio_candidates: archive.org — search 'Reina Valera 1909 audio' (several public-domain narrations of the 1909 text)
live.bible.is / Faith Comes By Hearing — 'Spanish Reina-Valera 1909' Non-Drama (confirm redistribution license, not just streaming)
approval_checklist: 1. The TEXT (RV1909) is public domain — no issue committing derived gold.
2. Pick a recording from audio_candidates and confirm it narrates the RV1909 text specifically (not RVR1960 or another version) — exact-text-match.
3. Confirm the recording's license permits download + research use (PD narration is cleanest; some FCBH/bible.is audio is stream-only — do NOT use unless redistribution is allowed).
4. Then set: audio_url = the actual file/stream URL, license = the confirmed license, matches_text_translation = true, approved = true.
5. Run: pwsh -File scripts/audio-download.ps1 (dry run) then -Execute. Only download_eligible (all five gates) sources download.
sources.evidence: Safest target to approve first: the RV1909 text is public domain and public-domain 1909 narrations exist, so an exact-match, freely-licensed pairing is achievable. Pre-filled with candidate sources; the four human-gated fields (audio_url, license, matches_text_translation, approved) remain for the operator per approval_checklist. Until then it is correctly NOT download_eligible.
